title: Failure Classification, Retry Budgets, Backoff, and Load Shedding · Contract Lens: Retry Observability Ownership
summary: Every exception is requeued immediately, permanent schema failures block healthy work, a database outage causes a retry storm, and attempt counts exist only in process memory. Planned artifact: Add and verify the retry observability ownership increment in failure taxonomy and bounded retry policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record original identity, attempt, reason, first and next time, owner, budget, terminal disposition, and causal metrics without high-cardinality leaks.
Pause or shed broad consumer work when a shared dependency is unavailable instead of scheduling delayed retries for every message.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Retry Observability Ownership · Theory Retry Observability Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1524 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify permanent and ambiguous outcomes before applying both attempt and age budgets; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Retry Observability Ownership contract for the supplied data.
